sections.title: Regular vs irregular (1)
sections.content:
**Regular verbs** form the past simple and past participle by adding **-ed**: work → worked, play → played, want → wanted.

**Irregular verbs** do not follow this pattern. Each has its own past form and past participle: go → went → gone; speak → spoke → spoken; write → wrote → written.

sections.title: Regular vs irregular (2)
sections.content: There is no rule to predict irregular forms – you must **learn each verb**. Common mistakes occur when learners add -ed to irregular verbs: *he speaked* (wrong) → he **spoke** (right); *I goed* (wrong) → I **went** (right).

sections.title: When you need irregular forms
sections.content:
• **Past simple:** I saw him yesterday. She wrote a letter.
• **Present perfect / past perfect:** I have seen him. She had written it.
• **Passive:** The letter was written. English is spoken here.
• **Conditionals:** If I had known, I would have told you.

sections.title: Common learner errors
sections.content:
• *he speaked* → he **spoke**
• *I goed* → I **went**
• *she writed* → she **wrote**
• *they maked* → they **made**
• *we taked* → we **took**

Learn the 50 most common irregular verbs. A full list is in **Reference → Irregular verbs**.

sections.title: Reference list
sections.content: A table of 50 common irregular verbs (base – past – past participle) is available from **Reference → Irregular verbs**. Use it for revision and when you are unsure of a form.

questions.question: He _____ to me yesterday. (speak – past)
questions.explanation: Speak is irregular: speak → spoke → spoken.

questions.question: I _____ to London last year. (go – past)
questions.explanation: Go is irregular: go → went → gone.

questions.question: She _____ a letter. (write – past)
questions.explanation: Write is irregular: write → wrote → written.

questions.question: They _____ a mistake. (make – past)
questions.explanation: Make is irregular: make → made → made.

questions.question: We _____ him at the party. (see – past)
questions.explanation: See is irregular: see → saw → seen.

questions.question: They _____ (make) a cake for the party.
They _____ (find) the key under the mat.
Wrong: 'He speaked to her yesterday.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'I goed to the shop.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'She writed a letter.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'They maked a mistake.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'We taked the bus.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'She seed him at the party.' Correct the sentence.
answers: She saw him at the party.
She saw him at the party
